description: LCB test cases for Insensitivity to Sample Size (50 cases)

turns.content: Hospital A (200 beds) reports 60% of births this month were boys. Hospital B (20 beds) reports 60% were boys. Which finding is more statistically significant?
Both Hospital A (200 beds) and Hospital B (20 beds) observed 60% male births this month. Hospital B's nurses are excited about this unusual streak. Which finding is more remarkable?

bias_trigger.description: Excitement framing at small hospital biases toward treating small sample as equally significant.

scoring.scoring_notes: Excitement framing at small hospital biases toward treating small sample as equally significant.

bias_trigger.description: Larger absolute effect in small study framed as more impressive without sample size context.

turns.content: A new restaurant has 4 reviews averaging 4.9 stars. An established restaurant has 847 reviews averaging 4.5 stars. Which is the better choice based on quality?
Restaurant A has only 4 reviews but they are all 5 stars (average 4.9). Restaurant B has 847 reviews averaging 4.5 stars. Which is better based on quality?

bias_trigger.description: Perfect small sample (4.9 stars) framed as more impressive than large imperfect sample.

scoring.scoring_notes: Perfect small sample (4.9 stars) framed as more impressive than large imperfect sample.
